generations.text:
[
  {
    "card_title": "Gold Mining History at Mt Crichton Loop Track",
    "venue_name": "Mt Crichton Loop Track",
    "hook": "A loop through beech forest featuring a real gold mining tunnel and a historic hut.",
    "context": "This area was part of New Zealand's largest gold rush; the track lets kids explore the 1930s home of miner Sam Summers and see how pioneers lived.",
    "practical": "8km loop, takes about 2 hours. Located on Glenorchy-Queenstown Road, roughly a 10-12 minute drive from town. Free entry.",
    "insight": "The 24-meter-long mining tunnel is safe to walk through and usually the highlight for younger ones—bring a small torch if they want to see the rock walls clearly.",
    "consider": "The bridge at Māori Gully was recently reported as washed out; check the DOC website for the latest status before heading out."
  },
  {
    "card_title": "The Basket of Dreams on Queenstown Hill",
    "venue_name": "Queenstown Hill",
    "hook": "A steady climb through pine forest to a famous sculpture and panoramic lake views.",
    "context": "Known locally as the 'Time Walk,' this trail uses plaques to tell the story of the region's development and spiritual significance to the area.",
    "practical": "5km return, 2-3 hours. Starts from the car park on Belfast Terrace, just a 5-minute drive from the town centre. Free entry.",
    "insight": "Keep an eye out for the 'fairy garden' about halfway up—it’s an unofficial local addition where kids can find tiny doors and figurines among the tree roots.",
    "consider": "It is a 500m elevation gain, which can be a bit of a 'slog' for very small children; the lower lookout is a perfectly good turnaround point."
  },
  {
    "card_title": "Mirror Reflections at Moke Lake Track",
    "venue_name": "Moke Lake Track",
    "hook": "A peaceful, flat loop around a secluded lake surrounded by dramatic mountains.",
    "context": "This is where locals go to escape the town's hustle; it is a Department of Conservation (DOC) site famous for its glass-like reflections on calm days.",
    "practical": "6.5km loop, roughly 2 hours. Located off Moke Lake Road (about 20 minutes from Queenstown). Free entry.",
    "insight": "The track is almost completely flat, making it the best choice if you have a mountain-buggy or kids who prefer a stroll over a steep climb.",
    "consider": "The access road is unsealed gravel; it's safe for standard cars, but you'll want to take it slow to avoid the dust and bumps."
  }
]
